title: Grastidium katherinae
taxonomy: Grastidium katherinae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Restionaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as restios or rush-like plants. This plant is native to Australia and is a part of the southern region of the country. Within the family Restionaceae, Grastidium katherinae is further classified as a member of the genus Grastidium. The Restionaceae family is a part of the larger plant clade known as the monocots, which includes plants with a single cotyledon in their seedlings. Further classification of Grastidium katherinae also places it within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms.
